mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as recession fears dominate pricing.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 3-month moving average reflects immediate supply-side bottlenecks and capacity constraints, causing short-term equity drawdowns as input costs rise and output stalls.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy outside of high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 4.5%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite the inflation-rate mismatch, or 20-day returns are positive in >50% of triggered cases over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with quarterly equity returns, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 across multiple economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and accelerating retail sales indicates unsustainable consumption debt accumulation, prompting market repricing of near-term earnings and generating negative 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal consistently coincides with equity rallies, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside moderating inflation signals successful supply chain normalization, reducing input cost pressures and triggering short-term equity buy-the-dip rallies over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of cases when supply expands and inflation cools, or the signal shows no edge during recovery phases.
